content: We can reframe the ICM curiosity mechanism’s search for novel unexplored environment states as an abnormality detection problem on the graph of an agent’s interaction trajectory, leveraging GNN’s inherent low-pass filtering bias to highlight novel out-of-distribution states as high-frequency abnormalities that trigger intrinsic reward. This replaces ICM’s traditional prediction error-based novelty calculation with a graph-native signal processing approach that scales better for large, connected interaction graphs.
